TITLE: chore: Central logging framework; migrate print() calls to logging
LABELS: roadmap, type:chore, size:L, phase:A-foundation
---
## Context

The codebase currently contains **307 `print()` calls and zero use of the stdlib `logging` module**. This is documented technical debt (`docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` → "Print Statements for Logging", currently around line 168): log levels cannot be controlled, output cannot be redirected or silenced, and debugging user-reported issues means asking them to copy console spam. It also blocks the follow-up error-handling cleanup (silent `except` sites need a `logger.exception` target to migrate to).

Quality goal served: **maintainability / operability** — a single switch (`--debug` / env var) turns diagnostic chatter on and off, and every log line carries its module name.

This issue introduces a central logging configuration module and mechanically migrates every `print()` in `src/` to a logger call, using the strict recipe below. **No user-facing behavior changes**: every `QMessageBox` stays exactly as it is.

## Current state

Verified per-file `print(` counts (via `grep -rc "print(" src/ --include="*.py"`), 307 total across 23 files. All paths relative to `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/`:

| File | print() count |
|---|---|
| controllers/image_controller.py | 52 |
| io/export_formats.py | 39 |
| dialogs/yolo_trainer.py | 25 |
| controllers/dino_controller.py | 25 |
| dialogs/dicom_converter.py | 22 |
| inference/dino_utils.py | 19 |
| controllers/io_controller.py | 19 |
| dialogs/slice_registration.py | 18 |
| inference/sam_utils.py | 13 |
| controllers/class_controller.py | 13 |
| controllers/project_controller.py | 12 |
| io/import_formats.py | 11 |
| dialogs/stack_interpolator.py | 11 |
| controllers/sam_controller.py | 7 |
| widgets/image_label.py | 4 |
| annotator_window.py | 4 |
| training/sam_dataset.py | 3 |
| controllers/yolo_controller.py | 3 |
| dialogs/project_search.py | 2 |
| dialogs/image_augmenter.py | 2 |
| core/torch_utils.py | 1 |
| controllers/sam_train_controller.py | 1 |
| controllers/annotation_controller.py | 1 |

Additionally, **12 `traceback.print_exc()` sites** (all become `logger.exception`): `controllers/dino_controller.py:242,276,306`, `controllers/sam_controller.py:119`, `controllers/class_controller.py:190`, `controllers/sam_train_controller.py:55`, `inference/sam_utils.py:217`, `dialogs/dino_merge_dialog.py:312`, `dialogs/image_patcher.py:72`, `dialogs/slice_registration.py:370`, `dialogs/dicom_converter.py:366`, `dialogs/stack_interpolator.py:497`.

`main.py` has **zero** prints; its `main()` (main.py:39-46) is where configuration will be wired. `docs/08_crosscutting_concepts.md` has a "Logging and Debug Output" section (~line 512) that documents the print-based status quo and must be rewritten.

## Proposed design

New module: `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/core/logging_config.py` (deliberately **not** `core/logging.py`, to avoid any confusion/shadowing with the stdlib module).

```python
"""Central logging configuration (see the Logging section in docs/08)."""
import logging
import os
import sys

_PKG = "digitalsreeni_image_annotator"

def configure(level=None):
    """Configure the package logger once. Idempotent — safe to call twice
    (tests, re-entry): a second call must not add a second handler."""
    root = logging.getLogger(_PKG)
    if root.handlers:          # already configured
        return root
    if level is None:
        debug = ("--debug" in sys.argv
                 or os.environ.get("IMAGE_ANNOTATOR_DEBUG", "") not in ("", "0"))
        level = logging.DEBUG if debug else logging.INFO
    handler = logging.StreamHandler()   # stderr
    handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter(
        "%(asctime)s %(levelname)-7s %(name)s: %(message)s", "%H:%M:%S"))
    root.addHandler(handler)
    root.setLevel(level)
    root.propagate = False
    return root

def get_logger(name):
    return logging.getLogger(name)
```

Wiring in `main.py` `main()`, as the **first** statement before `QApplication(sys.argv)`:
```python
from .core.logging_config import configure
configure()
```

Per migrated module, add at the top (adjust relative depth: `..core` from controllers/inference/io/training/dialogs/widgets, `.core` from annotator_window.py, `.logging_config` within core/):
```python
from ..core.logging_config import get_logger
logger = get_logger(__name__)
```
Because every module's `__name__` starts with `digitalsreeni_image_annotator.`, all loggers inherit the package handler/level automatically.

## Migration recipe (strict — apply mechanically)

| # | Pattern in code | Replacement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `print(f"[SAM] msg …")`, `print(f"[DINO] msg …")`, `print(f"[SAM-POINTS] …")` (bracket tags) | `logger.info("msg …")` / `logger.debug(…)` per rules 4–6 — **drop the tag**; the logger name already carries the module |
| 2 | `print(f"Error …: {e}")`, `print(f"Failed …: {str(e)}")` **inside an `except` block** | `logger.exception("Error …")` — drop the `{e}` interpolation; `.exception()` appends message + traceback |
| 3 | `traceback.print_exc()` | delete the line — the adjacent print becomes `logger.exception(...)` per rule 2 (if there is no adjacent print, write `logger.exception("<short context>")`). Remove `import traceback` when it becomes unused |
| 4 | Diagnostic chatter: array shapes/dtypes/min-max, TIFF tags/metadata dumps, `"open_project method called"`, SAM point-coordinate dumps, per-slice progress | `logger.debug(…)` |
| 5 | State changes a user might care about: `"Project auto-saved."`, `"SAM model loaded: …"`, `"Created N slices for …"`, `"Class renamed from … to …"`, `"Project opened successfully: …"` | `logger.info(…)` |
| 6 | `print("Warning: …")`, `"Skipped …"`, `"… not found"`, ignored/soft-failure notices (not in an `except`) | `logger.warning(…)` |
| 7 | A print directly followed by `return`/`return None` for a failed user action (e.g. `sam_utils.apply_sam_points`: `"No SAM model selected."` at inference/sam_utils.py:412) | `logger.warning(…)` |
| 8 | Any `QMessageBox.*`, `show_info`, dialog text | **unchanged** — this issue never touches user-visible dialogs |

When in doubt between debug/info, pick **debug** — the pre-migration console was spammy and the INFO default should be quiet enough for daily use.

## Implementation steps

1. Create `core/logging_config.py` as above; call `configure()` at the top of `main.py:main()`.
2. Migrate package by package, running `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v` after each: `core/` → `inference/` → `training/` → `io/` → `controllers/` → `widgets/` → `dialogs/` → `annotator_window.py`.
3. In each file: add the logger preamble, convert every `print(` per the recipe, remove `traceback.print_exc()` and now-unused `import traceback`.
4. Add `tests/unit/test_logging_config.py` (see Test plan).
5. Launch the app headless (`QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen python -m src.digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main`, close it) once normally and once with `--debug`; confirm INFO output is sparse and DEBUG shows the old chatter.
6. Run the full suite: `pytest tests/ -v`.
7. Update docs (see below), then the senior-reviewer gate.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `grep -rn "print(" src/ --include="*.py"` returns **zero** matches.
- [ ] `grep -rn "traceback.print_exc" src/` returns **zero** matches.
- [ ] `core/logging_config.py` exists; `configure()` is idempotent (double call adds no second handler) and is called in `main.py` before `QApplication` is created.
- [ ] `--debug` argv flag and `IMAGE_ANNOTATOR_DEBUG=1` env var both switch the package logger to DEBUG; default is INFO.
- [ ] Every migrated module uses `logger = get_logger(__name__)` (spot-check: `grep -rln "get_logger(__name__)" src/ | wc -l` ≥ 23).
- [ ] No `QMessageBox` call added, removed, or reworded (diff shows none).
- [ ] `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v` passes (includes the AST inline-import check, ADR-016) and `pytest tests/ -v` passes (94+ tests).

## Test plan

New file `tests/unit/test_logging_config.py` (plain pytest, no Qt needed):

- `test_configure_default_level_is_info` — `configure()` (with argv/env cleaned via monkeypatch) → package logger level INFO.
- `test_env_var_enables_debug` — `monkeypatch.setenv("IMAGE_ANNOTATOR_DEBUG", "1")`; fresh logger (remove handlers first) → DEBUG.
- `test_debug_flag_enables_debug` — `monkeypatch.setattr(sys, "argv", ["prog", "--debug"])` → DEBUG.
- `test_configure_is_idempotent` — call twice; `len(logger.handlers) == 1`.
- `test_get_logger_is_namespaced` — `get_logger("digitalsreeni_image_annotator.foo").parent` chains to the package logger.

Each test must reset the `digitalsreeni_image_annotator` logger's handlers in setup/teardown so ordering doesn't matter.

Commands: `pytest tests/unit/test_logging_config.py -v`, then `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`, then `pytest tests/ -v`.

## Docs to update

- `docs/08_crosscutting_concepts.md` — rewrite the "Logging and Debug Output" section (~line 512): describe `core/logging_config.py`, the level policy (debug = chatter, info = state changes, warning = soft failures, exception = tracebacks), the `--debug`/`IMAGE_ANNOTATOR_DEBUG` switches, and the rule "new code uses `get_logger(__name__)`, never `print()`".
- `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` — mark the "Print Statements for Logging" debt item resolved (keep the entry with a resolution note, matching how other resolved items in that file are handled).
- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — add a new ADR (use the next free ADR number) recording the decision: stdlib `logging` with a single package-level console handler, no third-party logging dependency, level switch via CLI flag/env var, print() banned in `src/`.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **Smoke test is mandatory after each package**: the AST-based `test_annotator_window_inline_imports_are_resolvable` catches stale relative imports inside function bodies — "a launch that looks clean is NOT sufficient — inline imports fail only when the function is called at runtime" (ADR-016). Adding the `from ..core.logging_config import get_logger` preamble at the wrong relative depth is exactly the class of bug it exists for.
- **Do not change QMessageBox behavior.** Several prints sit right next to dialogs (e.g. `controllers/sam_controller.py`, `controllers/project_controller.py`); migrate only the print.
- **Do not "improve" error handling while here.** Sites like `inference/sam_utils.py:365-366` (`except Exception: pass`) get their semantic fix in the follow-up error-handling issue (#34, which is blocked by this one). Here you only convert existing prints mechanically.
- Per-slice/per-file loop prints (e.g. `controllers/image_controller.py:create_slices`, export loops in `io/export_formats.py`) must go to `logger.debug`, not `info` — an INFO-level line per slice on a 2560-slice stack is unusable.
- `logger.exception(...)` may only be called inside an `except` block; outside one, use `logger.error(..., exc_info=True)` or plain `logger.error`.
- Keep f-string interpolation of *data* (names, counts, paths) — only drop exception objects from messages that become `logger.exception`.
- Inference guardrail (CLAUDE.md testing checklist): after migrating `inference/sam_utils.py` / `inference/dino_utils.py`, verify no code path that previously printed-and-returned now raises or silently drops the message — behavior must be identical apart from the output channel.

## Dependencies

None. (#34 is blocked by this issue.)

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
